description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Find and copy the link to the official Shipping Policy Template article
tasks.description: Use the Help Center search and filters to locate the most popular 'Shipping Policy Template' article under Store Setup, then copy its shareable URL. Success = the share link for the top 'Shipping Policy Template' article in the 'Store setup' filter is copied using the in-site 'Copy URL' control.
steps: From the main platform navigation, click the 'Help Center' link to open the Help Center homepage.
At the top of the Help Center, click inside the global search bar.
Type 'shipping policy template' into the search field and press Enter.
Below the search bar, locate the filter chips and click the 'Store setup' filter to narrow results.
On the search results page, click the sort dropdown and select 'Most popular'.
In the sorted results list, click the first article whose title contains the exact phrase 'Shipping Policy Template'.
On the article page, locate the 'Share' or link icon near the article title and click it.
In the share options panel, click the 'Copy URL to clipboard' button to copy the article’s direct link.

tasks.name: Use Help Center carrier comparison to select the fastest US shipping option under $10 for parcels under 2 kg
tasks.description: Navigate to the Shipping & Fulfillment help articles, open the carrier comparison guide, and select a carrier for US shipments weighing under 2 kg with a shipping cost under $10, prioritizing the shortest delivery time. Success = the comparison table row for the carrier that meets the weight limit, costs under $10, and appears first after sorting by fastest delivery is opened/selected.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the 'Shipping & Fulfillment' category tile or link.
In the Shipping & Fulfillment category page, scroll until you see an article titled 'Carrier comparison' or similar and click that article.
Within the carrier comparison article, scroll to the comparison table and click the region dropdown above the table.
From the region dropdown, select 'United States' as the shipping destination.
Locate the weight range filter next to the table and select the '0–2 kg' range.
Find the price filter field labeled 'Max cost' and type '10' to set a maximum shipping cost of $10.
Above the table, click the sort dropdown and choose 'Fastest delivery time' or 'Delivery time (Low to High)'.
In the filtered and sorted table, click on the first carrier row in the list, which represents the fastest carrier that costs under $10 for parcels under 2 kg.
On the carrier detail panel or page that opens, click any 'Use this carrier' or 'View setup instructions' button to open the setup instructions for that selected carrier.

tasks.name: Find a Shopify integration setup guide with a video under 10 minutes and start the tutorial
tasks.description: Use the Help Center to locate a Shopify integration article that includes a video tutorial with a duration of 10 minutes or less and start playing that video. Success = a Shopify integration article with a displayed video length of 10:00 minutes or less is opened and its embedded video is started using the in-page Play control.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the 'Store Integrations' category.
On the Store Integrations page, locate the integration filters and click the 'Shopify' filter or logo.
In the filtered list of Shopify-related articles, look for entries that have a video duration label displayed (for example, '8 min' or '10 min') next to the title.
Identify an article whose video duration label shows a length of '10 min' or less and click that article’s title.
On the article page, scroll until the embedded tutorial video player is fully visible.
If there is a video playlist or multiple videos, click the video that shows a duration of 10 minutes or less under its thumbnail.
Move your cursor over the selected video player and click the central 'Play' button icon to start the tutorial.
If a quality or speed control panel opens automatically, close it by clicking the 'X' or outside the panel so the tutorial plays normally.

tasks.name: Use Billing help docs to prepare a downgrade request from Pro to Basic with a $30/month budget
tasks.description: Find instructions on changing subscription plans, follow the in-article link to the contact form, and compose a downgrade request from Pro to Basic specifying a maximum budget of $30 per month. Success = a support request form with category 'Billing', topic 'Downgrade to Basic', and text mentioning '$30/month' is filled out and submitted or prepared using the site’s form controls.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click in the search bar at the top.
Type 'change subscription plan downgrade' into the search field and press Enter.
On the search results page, click the article titled 'Manage subscriptions and plan changes' or the closest match.
Within that article, scroll until you see a section about downgrading from a higher plan to a lower plan and locate a link labeled 'Contact support to change plan' or similar.
Click the 'Contact support to change plan' link to open the in-site support request form in a new page or panel.
In the support form, open the 'Category' dropdown and select 'Billing & subscriptions'.
Open the 'Topic' or 'Issue type' dropdown and select an option labeled 'Downgrade to Basic plan' or similar.
In the message or description text area, type a short request that explicitly includes the phrase 'downgrade from Pro to Basic' and states 'maximum budget is $30/month'.

Fill in any required non-email fields such as 'Account ID' or 'Store name' with placeholder values (for example, 'Account ID: 12345'), and click the 'Submit' or 'Preview request' button at the bottom of the form.

tasks.name: Enable auto-cancellation of unfulfilled orders after 7 days using a link from the Help Center
tasks.description: Search the Help Center for order automation, open the article about automatically cancelling unfulfilled orders, and use its embedded link to configure the setting so that orders are auto-cancelled after 7 days. Success = the order automation settings page is reached via the help article, and the 'Auto-cancel unfulfilled orders' rule is enabled with a time limit set to 7 days.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click on the search bar.
Type 'auto cancel unfulfilled orders' into the search field and press Enter.
On the search results page, click the article whose title includes the phrase 'Auto-cancel unfulfilled orders' or a close variant.
In the article, scroll until you find a section describing how to configure the auto-cancel rule and locate a button or link labeled 'Open order automation settings'.
Click the 'Open order automation settings' link to navigate to the order automation settings page inside the platform.
On the order automation settings page, locate the toggle or checkbox labeled 'Enable auto-cancel for unfulfilled orders' and click it to turn it on.
Find the numeric field or dropdown labeled 'Cancel after' or 'Unfulfilled for' and enter the value '7' or select '7 days' from the options.
Click the 'Save changes' or 'Update automation' button at the bottom of the settings section to apply the new 7-day auto-cancellation rule.

tasks.name: Create a canned response for defective item returns using a Help Center message template
tasks.description: Locate the Help Center article that provides message templates for returns, choose the template specific to defective items, and save it as a reusable canned response in the platform. Success = a new canned response named for defective returns is created using the defective-item template via the provided in-site controls.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the 'Customer Support & Messaging' category or its closest equivalent.
On the category page, scroll to find an article titled 'Return and refund message templates' or similar and click it.
Inside the article, locate the subsection labeled 'Defective item return template' and scroll until the full text of that template is visible.
Below or next to the defective item template, click the button labeled 'Create canned response from this template' or similar.
When the canned response creation form opens, click into the 'Title' or 'Name' field and type 'Defective item return - template A'.
In the response body editor, ensure the template text is present and, if there is a placeholder for a discount (e.g., '[DISCOUNT]'), replace it by typing '10% discount' directly into the editor.
In any available 'Category' or 'Folder' dropdown for the canned response, select or create a category named 'Returns & refunds'.
Click the 'Save canned response' or 'Create template' button at the bottom of the form to store this defective-item return message for later use.

tasks.name: Troubleshoot SYNC_409 product feed errors and report an incident via Help Center and Status pages
tasks.description: Use the Help Center to find guidance for the SYNC_409 product feed error, navigate to the system status page from that article, filter by Product Sync incidents, and submit an incident report marked with medium severity. Success = the incident report form accessed from the Status page is submitted or prepared with error code 'SYNC_409' and severity 'Medium'.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click in the search bar at the top.
Type 'SYNC_409 product feed error' into the search field and press Enter.
In the results, click the article whose title includes the exact code 'SYNC_409'.
Within the article, scroll to the section titled 'Check system status' or similar and click the link labeled 'View system status page'.
On the system status page, locate the service filter controls and click the filter or checkbox for 'Product Sync' or 'Catalog sync'.
In the incidents or events list, locate and click the most recent incident related to product synchronization if one is listed.
On the incident detail page or status page header, click the button labeled 'Report an issue' or 'Report incident'.
In the incident report form, type 'SYNC_409 product feed not updating' into the subject field.

In the severity dropdown, select 'Medium', then in the description text area, type a brief description including the text 'SYNC_409 error for product feed updates', and click the 'Submit report' button.

tasks.name: Adjust payout schedule to weekly with a $100 minimum threshold using instructions from the Help Center
tasks.description: Find documentation on payout schedules, follow the link to payout settings, and configure payouts to occur weekly whenever the account balance exceeds $100. Success = the payout settings page is reached via the help article and updated so that frequency is set to 'Weekly' and the minimum payout threshold is set to 100 USD.
steps: From the Help Center homepage, click the 'Payments & Payouts' category.
On the Payments & Payouts page, click the article titled 'Payout schedules and minimum thresholds' or the closest match.
In that article, scroll until you find a section showing how to change payout frequency and minimum amount, and click the link labeled 'Open payout settings' or similar.
On the payout settings page that opens, locate the dropdown labeled 'Payout frequency' and click it.
From the frequency options, select 'Weekly' as the payout schedule.
Find the input field labeled 'Minimum payout amount' or 'Threshold (USD)' and type '100' into the field.
If there is a currency selector next to the threshold field, ensure 'USD' is selected by clicking the dropdown and choosing 'USD'.
Click the 'Save payout settings' or 'Update schedule' button at the bottom of the page to apply the weekly payout with a $100 minimum threshold.
